briefdescription.para: A full code point was parsed successfully.
More bytes are required to decide.
The leading bytes are invalid or unsupported.
Status for parsing one UTF-8 code point from the front of a byte buffer.

memberdef.definition: using erbsland::cterm::impl::GetLargerIntT = typedef typename GetLargerInt<A, B>::type
memberdef.name: GetLargerIntT

memberdef.definition: using erbsland::cterm::impl::CommonIntT = typedef typename CommonInt<A, B>::type
memberdef.name: CommonIntT

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint32_t hash, const InputValue inputValue) noexcept
memberdef.name: hashCombineInput32

briefdescription.para: Combine an existing hash with a new input value with 32bit precision.

parameterdescription.para: The existing hash.
The new input value.

simplesect.para: The combined hash.

briefdescription.para: Create a 32bit hash for one or more integral values.

parameterdescription.para: The input values.

simplesect.para: The combined hash.

memberdef.argsstring: (const uint64_t hash, const InputValue inputValue) noexcept
memberdef.name: hashCombineInput64

briefdescription.para: Combine an existing hash with a new input value with 64bit precision.

parameterdescription.para: The existing hash.
The new input value.

simplesect.para: The combined hash.

briefdescription.para: Create a 64bit hash for one or more integral values.

parameterdescription.para: The input values.

simplesect.para: The combined hash.

briefdescription.para: Create an std::size_t hash for one or more integral values.

detaileddescription.para: This automatically chooses hash32 or hash64 depending on the platform.

briefdescription.para: Test if an integer value is negative.

detaileddescription.para:
This helper is safe to use with unsigned integers, where it always returns false.
T
Any signed or unsigned integer type.
value
The value to test.
true if the value is signed and below zero.

briefdescription.para: Get the absolute value of an integer as an unsigned integer.

detaileddescription.para:
This helper avoids undefined behavior for the minimum value of signed integer types.
T
Any signed or unsigned integer type.
value
The value to convert.
The absolute value represented as the matching unsigned integer type.

briefdescription.para: Get the saturated extreme value for a multiplication overflow.

detaileddescription.para:
The returned value is the minimum of A for a negative signed product, and the maximum of A otherwise.
A
The target integer type.
B
The second operand integer type.
a
The first multiplication operand.
b
The second multiplication operand.
The saturated extreme value for the product sign.

briefdescription.para: Add two integers but limit the result to the maximum possible values.

parameterdescription.para: Any signed or unsigned integer type
The first value.
The second value.

simplesect.para: The addition result, limited to the used type.

briefdescription.para: Subtract two integers but limit the result to the maximum possible values.

parameterdescription.para: Any signed or unsigned integer type
The first value.
The second value.

simplesect.para: The subtraction result, limited to the used type.

briefdescription.para: Multiply two integers but limit the result to the maximum possible values.

parameterdescription.para: Any signed or unsigned integer type.
The first multiplier.
The second multiplier.

simplesect.para: The product, limited to the used type.

briefdescription.para: Divide integers but limit the result to the maximum possible values.

parameterdescription.para: Any signed or unsigned integer type.
dividend.
divisor.

simplesect.para: The result of the division, limited to the used type.

briefdescription.para: Get the remainder of a division between integers but limit the result to the maximum possible values.

parameterdescription.para: Any signed or unsigned integer type.
dividend.
divisor.

simplesect.para: The result of the division, limited to the used type.

briefdescription.para: Test if an addition will overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The type for the operands.
The first value of the operation to test.
The second value of the operation to test.

simplesect.para: true if an overflow will occur, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test if a subtraction will overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The type for the operands.
The first value of the operation to test.
The second value of the operation to test.

simplesect.para: true if an overflow will occur, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test if a multiplication will overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The type for the operands.
The first value of the operation to test.
The second value of the operation to test.

simplesect.para: true if an overflow will occur, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test if a division will overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The type for the operands.
The first value of the operation to test.
The second value of the operation to test.

simplesect.para: true if an overflow will occur, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test if a modulo will overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The type for the operands.
The first value of the operation to test.
The second value of the operation to test.

simplesect.para: true if an overflow will occur, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Convert an integer type into another one, but make sure the result will not overflow.

detaileddescription.para:
If e.g. the unsigned 16bit value 0x2000 is casted to a unsigned 8bit value, the result is 0xff, which is the maximum possible for the target type. If a signed 8bit value -10 is cases to a unsigned 8bit value, the result is zero, because this is the smallest possible value for the type.
TargetType
The target type for the cast.
SourceType
The source type to cast.
value
The source value to cast.
A value as target type.

briefdescription.para: Check if a saturating cast will overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The target type for the cast.
The source type to cast.
The source value to check for an overflow.

simplesect.para: true if the case would overflow and change value.

briefdescription.para: Increment a value but never overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The type of the value.
The value to increment.

briefdescription.para: Increment a value but never overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The type of the value.
The value to decrement.

briefdescription.para: Saturated add with any integer type.

detaileddescription.para:
This will allow to use any integer type as second operand for a saturated addition. The result type will be the type of the first operand.
A
The target type for the operation.
B
The type of the second summand.
a
The value to change.
b
The value to add.
The result.

briefdescription.para: Saturated subtract with any integer type.

detaileddescription.para:
This will allow to use any integer type as second operand for a saturated subtraction. The result type will be the type of the first operand.
A
The target type for the operation.
B
The type of the subtrahend.
a
The value to change.
b
The value to subtract.
The result.

briefdescription.para: Saturated multiply with any compatible integer type.

detaileddescription.para:
This will allow to use (almost) any integer type as second operand. The result type will be the type of the first operand.
A
The target type for the operation.
B
The type of the factor.
a
The value to change.
b
The factor.
The result.

briefdescription.para: Saturated division with any compatible integer type.

detaileddescription.para: This will allow to use any integer type as second operand. The result type will be the type of the first operand.

parameterdescription.para: The target type for the operation.
The type of the divisor.
The value to change.
The divisor.

simplesect.para: The result.

briefdescription.para: Saturated modulo with any compatible integer type.

detaileddescription.para:
This will allow to use any integer type as second operand. The result type will be the type of the first operand.
A
The target type for the operation.
B
The type of the divisor.
a
The value to change.
b
The divisor.
The result.

briefdescription.para: Test if an addition will overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The target type of the operation to test for the overflow.
Operator type with no influence to the result type.
The first value of the operation to test.
The second value of the operation to test.

simplesect.para: true if an overflow will occur, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test if a subtraction will overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The target type of the operation to test for the overflow.
Operator type with no influence to the result type.
The first value of the operation to test.
The second value of the operation to test.

simplesect.para: true if an overflow will occur, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test if a multiplication will overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The target type of the operation to test for the overflow.
Operator type with no influence to the result type.
The first value of the operation to test.
The second value of the operation to test.

simplesect.para: true if an overflow will occur, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test if a division will overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The target type of the operation to test for the overflow.
Operator type with no influence to the result type.
The first value of the operation to test.
The second value of the operation to test.

simplesect.para: true if an overflow will occur, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Test if a modulo operation will overflow.

parameterdescription.para: The target type of the operation to test for the overflow.
Operator type with no influence to the result type.
The first value of the operation to test.
The second value of the operation to test.

simplesect.para: true if an overflow will occur, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Convert one ASCII uppercase character to lowercase.

parameterdescription.para: The character to normalize.

simplesect.para: The lowercase ASCII character, or the original character.

briefdescription.para: Convert ASCII uppercase characters in a UTF-32 string to lowercase.

parameterdescription.para: The text to normalize.

simplesect.para: The lower-case ASCII copy of the text.

briefdescription.para: Test if one code point is in the valid Unicode range.
Test if one code point is an ISO control code.

parameterdescription.para: The code point to test.

simplesect.para: true if the code point is a control code.

briefdescription.para: Test if one code point is safe for Char construction.

detaileddescription.para: This accepts any valid Unicode code point except control characters. New-line and tabs are allowed control-characters.

briefdescription.para: Return a safe replacement for invalid code-points.

simplesect.para: Space for all valid Unicode code points, otherwise the Unicode replacement character.

briefdescription.para: Return a safe code point for Char.

detaileddescription.para:
Valid Unicode code points are preserved, control codes are replaced with a space - to represent an "invisible" character. Invalid Unicode code points are replaced with the Unicode replacement character. New-line and tabs are allowed control-characters.
The safe code point for use in terminal strings.

briefdescription.para: Test whether one code point is accepted as a visible string character.

parameterdescription.para: The code point to test.

simplesect.para: true if the code point is preserved in a terminal string.

briefdescription.para: Normalize ASCII whitespace runs to single spaces.

parameterdescription.para: The text to normalize.

simplesect.para: The text with collapsed ASCII whitespace.

briefdescription.para: Normalize ASCII whitespace runs to single spaces in-place.

parameterdescription.para: The text to normalize.

simplesect.para: true if the original text contained only ASCII whitespace, otherwise false.

briefdescription.para: Test if the given UTF-32 string contains only ASCII whitespace.

parameterdescription.para: The text to examine.

simplesect.para: true if the text contains only ASCII whitespace.

briefdescription.para: Get the generated Unicode width table.

simplesect.para: A sorted table of contiguous Unicode ranges with console widths.

briefdescription.para: Get the terminal cell width for a single Unicode code-point.

parameterdescription.para: The Unicode code-point.

simplesect.para: The width in console cells: 0, 1 or 2.

briefdescription.para: Calculate the display width of the given string.

parameterdescription.para: The string to calculate the display width for. Invalid UTF-8 bytes are replaced with the Unicode replacement character.

simplesect.para: The number of "blocks" on the terminal.
